mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) trending upward signals robust domestic demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day SPX returns is negative or not statistically positive (p>0.10), or ranked IC over 2-year window < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) crossing a threshold within a short period indicates imminent recession risk, leading to de-risking by equity investors and negative forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward SPX return after UNRATE triggers is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or >40% of triggered instances produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, real interest rates become deeply negative, distorting capital allocation and creating a bubble-like environment that subsequently bursts, producing negative forward returns over a 20-day horizon after a lag.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=235.64, FEDFUNDS=0.08 at t-1mo implies negative real rate
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL growth * FEDFUNDS level) fails to have a statistically significant negative coefficient in a predictive regression of 20-day SPX returns (p>0.10) or out-of-sample rank IC < 0.03 over 2 years.

mechanism_narrative: During a sustained period of near-zero federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS), the economy's low rate environment drives equity valuations higher through compressed discount rates, favoring positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as long as the zero lower bound persists.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% for at least three consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.08 at t-1mo, already in ZLB
regime_hint: zero lower bound / unconventional monetary policy regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during ZLB periods are not statistically higher than non-ZLB periods (Mann-Whitney U test p>0.05) or rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Continued growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing sector, signaling economic strength and supporting corporate profits, leading to positive forward returns over a one-quarter horizon regardless of macroeconomic cycles.
failure_condition: The 12-month change in INDPRO shows no positive correlation with subsequent 60-day SPX returns (Pearson r < 0.10) or the signal produces significant negative returns in more than 40% of rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: A large monthly drop in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical threshold signals a sudden contraction in construction activity, which historically presages a broader economic slowdown and negative equity market returns over the subsequent week as investors reprice risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
regime_hint: post-financial crisis or housing downturn regime
failure_condition: After a HOUST trigger, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p>0.10) or the triggered frequency is less than 5 occurrences in any 5-year window making statistical test underpowered.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with stagnant or declining industrial production (INDPRO) reveals a productivity problem: hiring without output growth compresses profit margins, leading to a revaluation of equities downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of PAYEMS growth and INDPRO growth fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient in a multivariate regression on 20-day SPX returns (p>0.10) or the joint signal's rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is at elevated levels above the natural rate, a further increase signals deep economic distress and forces aggressive monetary easing; however, equity markets initially sell off on the bad news before rebounding, creating a short-term negative then longer-term positive profile. Over a 20-day horizon, the net effect is negative due to the initial panic.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.7 at t-1mo, already above 6% and may be rising
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns after UNRATE triggers are not significantly negative (one-sided test p>0.05) or the distribution of returns shows a mean positive return after the trigger, contradicting the net negative prediction.
